Title: Soybean steroids improve crop abiotic stress tolerance and increase yield

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves abiotic stress tolerance when expressed in transgenic hairy roots; contributes to activation of sterol-pathway target GmSQE1 as part of NF-Y complex
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: phytosterols and triterpenoids including fucosterol and soyasaponin II
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhance crop drought/osmotic stress tolerance and reduce oxidative damage
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: soybean seedlings/roots under stress; seeds also reflect improved quality under drought
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induces GmSQE1-associated stress response module; direct own expression pattern not fully detailed in this paper
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenic hairy roots under stress conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; LCI; GST pull-down; transgenic hairy-root assay; LUC promoter activation assay; EMSA as part of trimeric complex
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmNF-YA2 physically interacts with GmNF-YC9 and GmNF-YB24. 2. GmNF-YA2 overexpression in hairy roots enhances stress tolerance traits. 3. GmNF-YA2 contributes to transcriptional activation of GmSQE1 within the CCAAT-box NF-Y complex.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be combined with other NF-Y subunits or GmSQE1 to modulate sterol metabolism for stress-resilient soybean engineering
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a regulatory complex subunit, but not the principal focal regulator of the paper compared with GmNF-YC9.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves abiotic stress tolerance in transgenic hairy roots and supports activation of GmSQE1 as part of NF-Y complex
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: phytosterols and triterpenoids including fucosterol and soyasaponin II
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhance crop drought/osmotic stress tolerance and reduce oxidative damage
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: soybean seedlings/roots under stress; seeds also reflect improved quality under drought
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Associated with stress-responsive NF-Y complex; direct own expression pattern not fully detailed in this paper
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenic hairy roots under stress conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; LCI; GST pull-down; transgenic hairy-root assay; LUC promoter activation assay; EMSA as part of trimeric complex
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmNF-YB24 physically interacts with GmNF-YC9 and GmNF-YA2. 2. GmNF-YB24 improves stress tolerance in soybean hairy roots. 3. GmNF-YB24 participates in activation of the GmSQE1 promoter in the NF-Y complex.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component for NF-Y complex stacking to increase sterol-mediated abiotic stress tolerance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions as a complex subunit rather than the main decisive regulator emphasized by the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Squalene epoxidation step supplying 2,3-oxidosqualene to phytosterol and triterpenoid branches
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes squalene to 2,3-oxidosqualene, the precursor for cycloartenol- and beta-amyrin-derived sterols/triterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for phytosterols and triterpenoids including fucosterol and soyasaponin II
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting enzyme in steroid synthesis as stated in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression enhances soybean drought, salt, osmotic, heat and oxidative stress tolerance and increases yield under field drought; inhibition weakens stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: phytosterols and triterpenoids including fucosterol and soyasaponin II
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improve abiotic stress tolerance and reduce oxidative damage in crops
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Soybean seedlings, especially roots under mannitol stress; beneficial effects also reflected in seeds under field drought
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by drought and salt stress; elevated in GmNF-YC9-OE, GmNF-YA2 and GmNF-YB24 backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript abundance under drought and salt stress and in overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmNF-YA2; GmNF-YB24; GmNF-YC9 (transcriptional regulation complex targeting promoter)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in stable transgenic soybean; qRT-PCR; promoter-LUC assay; EMSA; enzyme activity ELISA; 2,3-oxidosqualene measurement; metabolomics; LC-MS metabolite quantification; inhibitor assay with terbinafine; field drought tests
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmSQE1 is directly activated by the GmNF-YA2/GmNF-YB24/GmNF-YC9 CCAAT-box complex. 2. GmSQE1 overexpression increases SQE activity, 2,3-oxidosqualene production, sterol/triterpenoid accumulation, and abiotic stress tolerance. 3. GmSQE1 overexpression improves agronomic performance and yield per plant under drought in field trials, whereas SQE inhibition reduces stress tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought; salt; mannitol-induced osmotic stress; methyl viologen; H2O2; high temperature; terbinafine inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pTF101 overexpression construct; promoter-LUC reporter using native GmSQE1 promoter
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious agronomic penalty under normal irrigation reported; high concentrations of downstream steroids can inhibit growth, implying dosage sensitivity of pathway outputs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for soybean engineering or selection to improve drought tolerance, oxidative stress resistance, and yield stability; can be stacked with upstream NF-Y regulators or combined with metabolite application strategies
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and metabolomics/UHPLC-MS-MS, no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Terbinafine inhibition caused squalene accumulation and reduced stress tolerance, supporting importance of flux to 2,3-oxidosqualene and downstream metabolites rather than squalene accumulation alone.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms trimeric CCAAT-box transcriptional activation complex with GmNF-YA2 and GmNF-YB24
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master upstream activator module that directly increases GmSQE1 expression and thereby boosts sterol/triterpenoid accumulation under abiotic stress
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Sterol/steroid and triterpenoid biosynthesis via regulation of precursor-forming rate-limiting step
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also upregulates multiple oxidative stress-related genes according to transcriptome discussion; no flowering-time phenotype observed in soybean under tested conditions despite homology to flowering-related NF-YCs
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: phytosterols and triterpenoids including fucosterol and soyasaponin II
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhance tolerance to drought, salt, osmotic and oxidative stresses; improve crop performance under drought
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Soybean seedlings under stress; roots for measured pathway intermediates; seeds show improved quality under field drought
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression enhances soybean stress tolerance, reduces oxidative damage, promotes accumulation of sterol metabolites, and increases yield per plant under drought
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly induced by drought and salt treatments
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in overexpression lines; stress-inducible in soybean
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression in soybean; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; Y2H; BiFC; LCI; GST pull-down; promoter-LUC assay; EMSA; metabolomics; physiological stress assays; field drought tests
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation through binding of NF-Y trimer to CCAAT-box in target promoter
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought; salt; mannitol; methyl viologen; H2O2; high temperature
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmNF-YC9 is stress inducible and its overexpression improves soybean tolerance to drought, salt, osmotic and oxidative stress. 2. GmNF-YC9 forms a CCAAT-box NF-Y complex with GmNF-YA2 and GmNF-YB24. 3. This complex directly activates GmSQE1, increasing SQE activity, 2,3-oxidosqualene, and downstream steroids/triterpenoids such as fucosterol and soyasaponin II.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate regulator for engineering stress-resilient soybean and potentially other crops through pathway activation; suitable for stacking with GmSQE1 or chemical steroid application
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear penalty under normal irrigation reported; possible pleiotropy with flowering regulation remains unresolved

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Component of trimeric CCAAT-box complex with GmNF-YB24 and GmNF-YC9
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Co-activator required within NF-Y complex for activation of GmSQE1 and sterol-pathway regulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: phytosterols and triterpenoids including fucosterol and soyasaponin II
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hairy-root expression improves abiotic stress tolerance and raises GmSQE1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; LCI; pull-down; hairy-root transformation; LUC assay; EMSA in complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as NF-Y complex subunit
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GmSQE1 promoter as part of trimeric complex
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmNF-YA2 interacts with GmNF-YC9 and GmNF-YB24. 2. GmNF-YA2 contributes to abiotic stress tolerance in soybean hairy roots. 3. GmNF-YA2 participates in activation of GmSQE1 within the NF-Y transcription complex.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary regulator for combinatorial engineering of sterol-mediated stress tolerance

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Component of trimeric CCAAT-box complex with GmNF-YA2 and GmNF-YC9
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Co-activator within NF-Y complex supporting direct activation of GmSQE1 and downstream sterol accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: phytosterols and triterpenoids including fucosterol and soyasaponin II
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hairy-root expression improves abiotic stress tolerance and elevates GmSQE1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; LCI; pull-down; hairy-root transformation; LUC assay; EMSA in complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as NF-Y complex subunit
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GmSQE1 promoter as part of trimeric complex
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmNF-YB24 interacts with GmNF-YC9 and GmNF-YA2. 2. GmNF-YB24 contributes to abiotic stress tolerance in soybean hairy roots. 3. GmNF-YB24 participates in activation of GmSQE1 within the NF-Y complex.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary regulator for combinatorial engineering of sterol-mediated stress tolerance
